items.title: EToro invests in onchain derivatives platform Extended as brokers race into DeFi
items.description: The broker plans to bring perpetual futures into Zengo wallet and expand DeFi products to its core platform as rivals like Robinhood expand onchain offerings.

items.title: Bitwise says STRC selloff signals crypto cycle nearing a bottom, not Strategy’s breaking point
items.description: Bitwise said STRC's volatility reflects a late-cycle leverage unwind, with institutions poised to replace Strategy as bitcoin's biggest buyer.

items.title: US Treasury sanctions over 100 ISIS-K crypto addresses that moved over $1.4 million
items.description: ISIS-K allegedly used its media wing to solicit donations via Tron, Monero, and Bitcoin, highlighting stablecoin issuers' growing role in sanctions enforcement.

items.title: SBI Crypto to shut down mining pool that holds roughly 2% of Bitcoin's hashrate
items.description: Miners must redirect their hashrate before the July 31 cutoff, as the pool will stop accepting contributions after that date.

items.title: Ondo Finance debuts SEC-aligned tokenized stock model with BlackRock ETF, Micron shares
items.description: Broadridge and transfer agent Oasis Pro underpin a new structure that keeps tokenized securities within existing U.S. market rules, based on the SEC's third-party custodial model.

items.title: JPMorgan says Strategy's bitcoin sales policy adds 'two-way risk' to crypto markets
items.description: The bank said Strategy's bitcoin sales policy adds avoidable market uncertainty and should be replaced with equity issuance to build cash reserves.

items.title: U.S. payroll growth slowed sharply in June, with only 57,000 jobs added
items.description: This morning's data could slow market expectations for a Fed rate hike as soon as this summer or early Fall.

items.title: A struggling Nasdaq-listed company that tried to copy Saylor's Bitcoin playbook is completely dumping crypto for AI
items.description: The Nasdaq-listed Korean media company once lined up $1 billion in financing to buy 10,000 bitcoin. A recent filing confirms its balance is now zero as it pivots to AI infrastructure and fights to stay listed.

items.title: Bitcoin price taps new July high above $62K on weak US jobs data
items.description: Bitcoin took daily gains to nearly 4% on the second day of "green July" as US labor-market signals supported an easing inflation policy from the Federal Reserve.

items.title: FBI director discloses Strategy holdings months after deadline: Report
items.description: Strategy is a registered US government contractor, though Kash Patel claimed that “no current conflict exists” with his $100,001 and $250,000 worth of the company’s stock that he reported late.

items.title: Ondo expands tokenized equities with onchain shareholder voting
items.description: Ondo's latest partnership adds shareholder governance to tokenized stocks as competition in blockchain-based equity offerings accelerates.

items.title: The biggest blockchain upgrades still to come in 2026
items.description: From Ethereum’s Glamsterdam to Solana’s Alpenglow, 2026’s most important developments are protocol upgrades, not price charts.

items.title: UK payments blueprint outlines tokenized payments for ‘multi-money ecosystem’
items.description: UK regulators published an update to a national retail payments blueprint, calling for infrastructure support for tokenization and interoperability with new forms of digital money.

items.title: Aave brings V3 lending and GHO stablecoin to Monad
items.description: Aave launched its V3 lending protocol on Monad with 12 supported assets as the network commits $15 million in first-year incentives to build liquidity and adoption.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Standard Chartered, Circle bring USDC minting onto banking rails
items.description: Standard Chartered and Circle launch bank-led USDC minting and redemption for institutions, starting in Dubai’s DIFC with planned global expansion.

items.title: Job seekers giving up: Labor force participation rate falls to lowest in 50 years, outside of Covid era
items.description: A drop in the unemployment rate helped provide some upside to what was an otherwise downbeat jobs report — but it was for all the wrong reasons.

items.title: Tesla stock sinks 7% despite strong deliveries report
items.description: Tesla is trying to recover from consecutive annual declines in vehicle sales that were partly caused by a consumer backlash against CEO Elon Musk.

items.title: Led by Buc-ee’s and new rival Dolly Parton, America’s gas station chains are in a mega-sizing era
items.description: Gas station chains led by Buc-ee's are coming closer to rivaling America's big box stores in size, and Dolly Parton is getting in on the retail trend.

items.title: Microsoft commits $2.5 billion and 6,000 employees to new AI implementation unit
items.description: Microsoft is the latest tech company to form a business focused on helping customers understand and implement artificial intelligence.

items.title: Sandwich chain Jersey Mike's files for IPO, reports 50% same-store sales growth in recent years
items.description: Jersey Mike's has nearly 3,300 locations, making it the second-largest hoagie sandwich chain in the U.S. behind Subway.

items.title: Married Americans tend to be wealthier and happier. Gen Z is forgoing the institution anyway
items.description: Gen Z defines the American Dream as having financial stability and being happy. Marriage could help them reach both of those goals, but many aren't sold on it.

items.title: Premier Lacrosse League plans to bring in team owners by 2028 'or soon thereafter,' co-founder says
items.description: Paul Rabil told CNBC he's banking on the 2028 Los Angeles Summer Games, where lacrosse will return as an Olympic sport, to shine a spotlight on the PLL.

items.title: Autonomous drone startup Quantum Systems raises $1.2 billion as investors pile into defense
items.description: Defense tech startups are raising record amounts on both sides of the Atlantic amid a flurry of investment in the sector.

items.title: Strategy’s bitcoin trading policy creates risks for broader markets, says JPMorgan
Bybit Card Launches in Peru: Seamless Spending with Up to 120 USDT in Rewards

items.description: Hiring slows after 3-month streak of overperformance

items.title: Trump’s $1bn plan to remake America’s capital
items.description: The US president calls it a ‘gift’ to the nation. Critics say he is racing past checks to stamp his mark on the capital

items.title: Americans brace for costly July 4 as Trump’s tariffs and Iran war bite
items.description: Food and gasoline prices elevated as country marks 250th birthday

items.title: On America’s 250th, remember your Adam Smith . . .
items.description: The ground is fertile for a renewal of the commitment to free markets and free people

items.title: OpenAI proposes handing Trump administration 5% stake
items.description: Sam Altman’s start-up in early talks for a public ownership deal as political pressure rises

items.title: Altman’s AI safety proposal: let us win, or everybody loses
items.description: A US-led world order would hand a big advantage to an American oligopoly that includes his own company

items.title: Apollo struggles to sell Hispanic grocer after US immigration raids hit sales
items.description: Investors and analysts say customers’ fear of Trump’s deportation campaign weakened the private equity-owned chain

items.title: Spain immigration amnesty draws 1.2mn applicants
items.description: More than twice the expected number of unauthorised immigrants applied for legal status, mostly from Latin America

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Mut3NTl36xc! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Why Tesla’s stock is sinking toward its worst day in a year despite blowout delivery numbers
items.description: The automaker shipped 480,126 EVs to consumers last quarter — much more than even bullish analysts projected.

items.title: ‘We were stunned’: My daughter, 39, said her mother-in-law gives her more money than we do. Do I call her out?
items.description: “She thanked us for the $100 birthday gift and then announced that her mother-in-law had sent her $400.”

items.title: I have no kids. Will I cause family drama by leaving different amounts to my nieces and nephews?
items.description: “Part of me would prefer to leave more to those for whom the money could make a meaningful difference.”

items.title: If you lost money on Trump crypto ventures, don’t whine
items.description: It should be no surprise the president made $1 billion on cryptocurrency deals that handed losses to so many who invested.

items.title: Palantir’s stock bounces back as analyst cheers company’s unique AI advantage
items.description: The software company’s shares have gotten off to a strong start this month following a weak run in June.

items.title: My doctor friend objected to his hospital’s fundraising tactics. Did management go too far?
items.description: “This shocking program was exploiting doctors and the trust inherent in their relationships with patients.”

items.title: How you as an investor can get ahead of the next AI investment wave
items.description: Also in Weekend Reads: Eldercare and family relationships, stock-market prospects for the second half of 2026, and how to set your summer streaming budget.

items.title: Where can I invest my kid’s ‘Trump account’ money? The Treasury Department just answered that question.
items.description: The money in “Trump accounts” has to be invested in low-cost index funds — and now parents and investors are learning which funds they can actually use.

items.title: Can Kazakhstan’s oil boom survive Putin’s War? | FT Film

1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: US economy undershoots forecasts with 57,000 jobs added in June
items.description: Hiring slows after 3-month streak of overperformance

items.title: Modi and Takaichi meet in the shadow of Chinese pressure
items.description: Japan and India draw closer after falling foul of Beijing’s restrictions on rare earth exports

items.title: The faltering engine of Germany’s auto industry
items.description: VW is a test of the country’s ability to adapt

items.title: Pubs to stay open until 5am for England’s World Cup match
items.description: Keir Starmer bows to pressure to allow an all-night party — or wake, depending on the result

items.title: The chip rally is tightening tech’s grip on Wall Street
items.description: AI spending frenzy has powered a semiconductor surge

items.title: Apollo struggles to sell Hispanic grocer after US immigration raids hit sales
items.description: Investors and analysts say customers’ fear of Trump’s deportation campaign weakened the private equity-owned chain

items.title: Teenage rapists given stricter sentence after public outcry
items.description: Boys handed a four-year detention sentence after attorney-general’s referral to the Court of Appeal

items.title: Vatican schism puts Maga Catholics under pressure
items.description: Pope Leo excommunicates ultra-conservative bishops whose views resonate with parts of the US right

items.title: To Reduce Electrical Grid Strain Amid Heat Wave, Data Centers Are Ordered to Use Backup Power
items.description: As triple-digit temperatures engulf much of the United States, the Trump administration wants grid managers to require the use of backup power that often goes unused.

items.title: What Private Credit Is, and Why Investors Are So Worried About It
items.description: Some say the industry expanded too quickly and extended loans to companies that won’t be able to pay them back.

items.title: What to know about the report.
Private Credit Can’t Stop the ‘Freak Out’
items.description: On Thursday, Blue Owl reported another quarter of significant investor withdrawal requests from some of its private credit funds.

items.title: Tesla Sales Surge 25% on Recovery in Europe
items.description: Price cuts helped the electric automaker rebound in Europe in the second quarter, offsetting declines in the United States.

items.title: Why the Jobs Market Has Wall Street and Washington on Edge
items.description: A rebound in hiring has put more pressure on the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates. New jobs numbers are scheduled for release on Thursday.

items.title: US Job Market Makes Slower but Steady Gains, June Employment Data Shows
items.description: Employers added fewer jobs in June than a month earlier but the unemployment rate ticked down, a decent showing for the U.S. economy.

items.title: Latest Jobs Report Shows Labor Market Is Not a Source of Inflationary Pressure
items.description: The unemployment rate ticked down to 4.2 percent, but average hourly earnings were steady, giving Kevin Warsh, the Federal Reserve chairman, room to focus on fighting inflation.
